openmeter/app/custominvoicing/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL persistence adapter for the custominvoicing app — implements appcustominvoicing.Adapter by reading and writing AppCustomInvoicing and AppCustomInvoicingCustomer Ent entities with soft-delete semantics and idempotent upserts.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping on every method
patterns.description: Every exported method body wraps Ent access in entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue so it rebinds to the caller's ctx-bound transaction.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) error { return tx.db.AppCustomInvoicing.Update().Where(...).SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()).Exec(ctx) })

patterns.name: Soft-delete everywhere
patterns.description: Records are never hard-deleted. Mutations set SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()); all queries add DeletedAtIsNil() predicate.
patterns.example: tx.db.AppCustomInvoicing.Update().Where(appcustominvoicing.ID(id), appcustominvoicing.DeletedAtIsNil()).SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()).Exec(ctx)

patterns.name: Upsert via OnConflictColumns
patterns.description: Create operations use .OnConflictColumns(...).UpdateNewValues() for idempotent upserts without a read-then-write.
patterns.example: .OnConflictColumns(appcustominvoicing.FieldID, appcustominvoicing.FieldNamespace).UpdateNewValues().Exec(ctx)

patterns.name: Tx/WithTx/Self triad
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx() (HijackTx + NewTxDriver), WithTx() (NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self() to enable entutils.TransactingRepo transaction rebinding.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txDb := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txDb.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: var _ appcustominvoicing.Adapter = (*adapter)(nil) at package level ensures the adapter satisfies the interface at compile time.
patterns.example: var _ appcustominvoicing.Adapter = (*adapter)(nil)

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() constructor
patterns.description: Constructor accepts a Config struct; Validate() checks all required fields before constructing the adapter.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (appcustominvoicing.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Zero-value return on NotFound
patterns.description: GetAppConfiguration and GetCustomerData return an empty struct (not an error) when the Ent query returns IsNotFound — callers rely on this zero-value sentinel.
patterns.example: if db.IsNotFound(err) { return custominvoicing.Configuration{}, nil }

key_file_guides.role: Adapter constructor, Config, and transaction plumbing (Tx/WithTx/Self). Foundation for all other files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not bypass WithTx/Self — other files rely on TransactingRepo rebinding through these methods.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD for app-level configuration (AppCustomInvoicing entity): GetAppConfiguration, UpsertAppConfiguration, DeleteAppConfiguration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetAppConfiguration returns empty Configuration{} (not an error) on NotFound — callers rely on this zero-value sentinel.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD for per-customer data (AppCustomInvoicingCustomer entity): GetCustomerData, UpsertCustomerData, DeleteCustomerData.
key_file_guides.watch_for: input.Validate() is called before entering the transaction — validation errors must not be wrapped in a transaction.

anti_patterns: Using a.db directly in a method body without TransactingRepo — falls off ctx-bound transactions
Hard-deleting rows instead of setting DeletedAt
Returning an error on NotFound from GetAppConfiguration or GetCustomerData — callers expect zero-value returns
Storing *entdb.Tx as a struct field instead of using the Tx/WithTx/Self triad

decisions.rationale: Preserves audit trail for billing apps; aligns with the rest of the billing domain's deletion convention.

decisions.decision: Tx/WithTx/Self triad instead of passing *entdb.Tx explicitly
decisions.rationale: Keeps transaction plumbing implicit in ctx; entutils.TransactingRepo handles rebinding without leaking *entdb.Tx into every call site.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new adapter method that writes a record inside the caller's transaction

openmeter/app/custominvoicing/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP handler layer for the custominvoicing app — exposes DraftSyncronized, IssuingSyncronized, and UpdatePaymentStatus endpoints by decoding API requests, delegating to appcustominvoicing.SyncService, and encoding billing.StandardInvoice responses via the shared httptransport pattern.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs per endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint is a method returning a typed HandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params] with inline decoder, operation, and encoder functions.
patterns.example: return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoderFn, operationFn, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Resp](http.StatusOK), httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("X"), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()))...)

patterns.name: Four type aliases per endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint block declares four type aliases: *Request (domain input), *Response (api type), *Params (path/query params), *Handler (typed handler alias).
patterns.example: type DraftSyncronizedRequest = appcustominvoicing.SyncDraftInvoiceInput; type DraftSyncronizedResponse = api.Invoice; type DraftSyncronizedParams = struct{ InvoiceID string }; type DraftSyncronizedHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...]

patterns.name: Namespace resolved from context in decoder
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first; on failure it returns an empty request and wraps the error.
patterns.example: namespace, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return DraftSyncronizedRequest{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace: %w", err) }

patterns.name: input.Validate() at operation func entry
patterns.description: The operation function validates the decoded request before calling the service — not inside the decoder.
patterns.example: func(ctx context.Context, request DraftSyncronizedRequest) (DraftSyncronizedResponse, error) { if err := request.Validate(); err != nil { return DraftSyncronizedResponse{}, err } ... }

patterns.name: Shared errorEncoder() attached to every handler
patterns.description: errors.go defines errorEncoder() with a chain of HandleErrorIfTypeMatches covering all billing error types; attached via httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()).
patterns.example: commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches[billing.NotFoundError](ctx, http.StatusNotFound, err, w, billing.EncodeValidationIssues) || commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches[billing.ValidationError](...)

patterns.name: Response mapped via billinghttpdriver.MapStandardInvoiceToAPI
patterns.description: All three endpoints return api.Invoice by delegating to billinghttpdriver.MapStandardInvoiceToAPI — never inline-constructing the API type.
patterns.example: return billinghttpdriver.MapStandardInvoiceToAPI(invoice)

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface definition, handler struct, New() constructor, and resolveNamespace helper. Entry point for wiring.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SyncService is the only service dependency — do not inject billing.Service directly here.

key_file_guides.role: Three endpoint handler methods: DraftSyncronized, IssuingSyncronized, UpdatePaymentStatus.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each method returns a new handler instance on every call — they are not cached on the struct.

key_file_guides.role: Centralized error encoder shared by all three handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New billing error types must be added here — missing an error type causes 500s instead of correct status codes.

key_file_guides.role: API to domain type conversions: mapUpsertStandardInvoiceResultFromAPI, mapFinalizeStandardInvoiceResultFromAPI, mapPaymentTriggerFromAPI.
key_file_guides.watch_for: mapPaymentTriggerFromAPI uses a switch with a default returning GenericValidationError — new API trigger values must be added to this switch.

anti_patterns: Injecting billing.Service directly into handler — use SyncService as the single dependency
Inline-constructing api.Invoice in operation functions — always delegate to billinghttpdriver.MapStandardInvoiceToAPI
Skipping request.Validate() in the operation function
Adding new billing error types without updating errorEncoder() in errors.go
Caching handler instances on the struct — each endpoint method must return a new handler

decisions.decision: SyncService as the sole handler dependency
decisions.rationale: Keeps the HTTP layer thin — all business logic lives in the service layer, not the handler.

decisions.decision: Shared errorEncoder() instead of per-handler error mapping
decisions.rationale: All three endpoints expose the same billing error surface; centralising avoids divergence.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new endpoint GetInvoiceStatus that decodes an invoiceId path param and returns api.Invoice

openmeter/app/custominvoicing/service.purpose: Business logic layer for the custominvoicing app — implements appcustominvoicing.Service (factory, customer data, and sync sub-interfaces) by orchestrating appcustominvoicing.Adapter, app.Service, and billing.Service within explicit transactions.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wrapping multi-step writes
patterns.description: Operations that touch multiple entities run inside transaction.Run or transaction.RunWithNoValue to ensure atomicity.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (app.AppBase, error) { appBase, _ := s.appService.CreateApp(...); s.adapter.UpsertAppConfiguration(...); return appBase, nil })

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() + compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Service constructor takes a Config struct; Validate() checks all required fields. var _ appcustominvoicing.Service = (*Service)(nil) enforces interface at compile time.
patterns.example: var _ appcustominvoicing.Service = (*Service)(nil)

patterns.name: ValidateInvoiceApp guard before billing mutations
patterns.description: sync.go methods call s.ValidateInvoiceApp(invoice) after fetching the invoice to assert it belongs to the custom invoicing app before firing triggers.
patterns.example: if err := s.ValidateInvoiceApp(invoice); err != nil { return billing.StandardInvoice{}, err }

patterns.name: input.Validate() at method entry
patterns.description: Every exported service method calls input.Validate() before any business logic or transaction.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return billing.StandardInvoice{}, err }

patterns.name: Double read around billing trigger in HandlePaymentTrigger
patterns.description: HandlePaymentTrigger does get → trigger → get: the second GetStandardInvoiceById is needed to observe post-trigger state and detect critical ValidationIssues.
patterns.example: invoice, _ = s.billingService.GetStandardInvoiceById(ctx, ...); criticalIssues := lo.Filter(invoice.ValidationIssues, func(i billing.ValidationIssue, _ int) bool { return i.Severity == billing.ValidationIssueSeverityCritical })

patterns.name: Critical ValidationIssue check causes transaction rollback
patterns.description: HandlePaymentTrigger returns billing.ValidationError if any critical issues are present after the trigger — this causes the wrapping transaction.Run to roll back.
patterns.example: if len(criticalIssues) > 0 { return billing.StandardInvoice{}, billing.ValidationError{Err: criticalIssues.AsError()} }

patterns.name: clock.Now() for testable timestamps
patterns.description: SyncDraftInvoice and SyncIssuingInvoice inject metadata timestamps using clock.Now(), not time.Now(), for deterministic testing.
patterns.example: AdditionalMetadata: map[string]string{appcustominvoicing.MetadataKeyDraftSyncedAt: clock.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)}

key_file_guides.role: Service struct definition, Config, Validate, New constructor. Sets up the three dependencies: adapter, appService, billingService.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service struct is exported (uppercase) unlike most sibling packages — intentional to allow direct *Service injection in Wire provider sets.

key_file_guides.role: SyncService implementation: SyncDraftInvoice, SyncIssuingInvoice, HandlePaymentTrigger, ValidateInvoiceApp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HandlePaymentTrigger does get-trigger-get — do not collapse the two reads; the second is required to observe post-trigger state.

key_file_guides.role: FactoryService implementation: CreateApp (two-step: appService.CreateApp + adapter.UpsertAppConfiguration), DeleteApp, UpsertAppConfiguration, GetAppConfiguration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateApp wraps both steps in a single transaction.Run — if adapter.UpsertAppConfiguration fails, the app row is rolled back.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerDataService implementation: thin delegation to adapter wrapped in transaction.Run/RunWithNoValue.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No business logic here — validation belongs in input types, not this file.

anti_patterns: Calling time.Now() directly instead of clock.Now() — breaks deterministic tests
Skipping ValidateInvoiceApp before billing mutations — allows cross-app invoice mutations
Not wrapping multi-entity writes in transaction.Run — risks partial writes on appService + adapter calls
Returning an error for NotFound from adapter.GetAppConfiguration — the adapter returns zero value; callers expect that
Adding billing.Service calls directly in customerdata.go — customer data operations must stay adapter-only

decisions.rationale: Allows Wire to inject *Service directly when both FactoryService and SyncService sub-interfaces are needed at different wiring sites.

decisions.decision: ValidateInvoiceApp is a standalone exported method
decisions.rationale: Used both internally (before triggers) and passed as InvoiceValidator callback to billing.Service sync methods, requiring a stable method signature.

decisions.decision: Critical-issue check causes transaction rollback in HandlePaymentTrigger
decisions.rationale: Ensures the state-machine trigger and any resulting side-effects are atomically reverted if the invoice ends up in an invalid state.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new SyncService method that fires a billing trigger and validates the post-trigger invoice state
